topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Sweet spot of my 50/25/25 weighting at 1.07: Consensus #8 Tier 2 (CLE landing spot), RSP DoT 86.3 (#5 overall, Starter grade), and MFL ADP 13.57 (#20). Roster has 7 WRs but several on expiring deals (6 contracts up); a flanker/slot starter with return-game upside is the cleanest BPA-meets-future-need pick if Mendoza/Sadiq are off the board.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #7 Tier 2 (SEA), RSP DoT 83.5 with 'ceiling as high as RBs 2-5' note, ADP 16.05. RB is my thinnest skill room (5) and Price's three-board confluence at 1.07 fits medium-RSP weighting. Seattle backfield offers real touches; not a reach by any of my three inputs.

topTargets.name: Kenyon Sadiq
topTargets.reasoning: Highest Consensus-ranked player likely to be on the board: #5 Tier 2 (NYJ, TE1), confirmed by ADP 13.11. RSP is cooler at DoT 82.3 (#30) but Brock Bowers high-end comp keeps it defensible. With 4 TEs already rostered I'd only take him if Concepcion and Price are gone — he's the consensus floor pick.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #6 Tier 2 (LV1, QB1), RSP DoT 85.6 (#7, RSP QB1), ADP 11.39. With only 2 QBs rostered and 6 contracts expiring, a Tier-2 QB1 with all three boards aligned is hard to pass at 1.07. Salary risk on a QB is the main concern given $4.1M cap room.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Round 2 target at 2.17. RSP DoT 83.9 with Jayden Reed comp ('worth second-round consideration and a potential bargain'), Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WAS), ADP 29.06 — all three signals converge in late round 2. Medium-RSP weighting nudges me toward this kind of well-scored Tier-4 over a sliding name.

capPosture: Tight at $40.9M used / $4.1M space — round 1 rookie's $1.2M base pushes me close, so 1.07 likely starts on taxi (50% hit) and 2.17 definitely goes taxi; 6 expiring contracts give me real flexibility next offseason if I hit on the picks.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: If I go Concepcion or Price at 1.07, Stowers at 2.17 is a sneaky Round-2 fit: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP DoT 84.4 ('on cusp of Starter tier'), ADP 21.25. Already 4 TEs but several on expiring deals; classic move-TE in Philly is the kind of upside swing a medium-RSP GM justifies in round 2.

summary: Tight cap and a stale-but-deep roster (22/22, 6 expiring contracts) push me toward best-skill-available with future-asset framing rather than chasing a contention-window QB. At 1.07 I'm targeting the player where my 50/25/25 weighting converges — KC Concepcion is the cleanest hit, with Price (RB need) and Sadiq (Consensus floor) as fallbacks. Round 2 at 2.17 is a taxi-stash lottery ticket on a Williams/Stowers-type whose RSP and ADP both validate the Consensus-tier they sit in.
